nodes.desc: User uploads a project-document PDF (or types activity details) on the Activity Forecasting page. Rendered by the upload submodule of the pages/activity_forecasting/ package.
Streamlit entrypoint. Renders the sidebar nav over 7 pages and loads the model + training data once at startup via model_loader.
Loads the trained Random Forest + ExtraTrees ensemble, per-org baseline, start-year correction, feature medians, metadata, and training data from data/rating_model_outputs at startup.

Trained Random Forest + ExtraTrees delta regressors (model.pkl, extra_model.pkl), plus per-org baseline (per_org_baseline.json), start-year correction (start_year_correction.json), feature medians (train_medians.json), and feature names (feature_names.json) produced offline by the forecasting model-training pipeline.

Historical IATI activity features + ratings. Used as the model's reference distribution, for performance metrics, and as the RAG retrieval corpus. Consumed in several bands, so it is exported via the ✳ proxy convention (a small 'train_features.csv ✳' chip beside each importer) rather than long lines.
train_features.csv is exported via the ✳ proxy convention: each importer is drawn with a small 'train_features.csv ✳' chip beside it rather than long lines from this file.
Saved UMAP reducers that compress target/summary embeddings into a small set of embedding features.
Per-outcome-tag classifiers produced offline by the forecasting model-training pipeline. Loaded at runtime by tag_model_loader.py and consumed by tag_predictor to score individual success dimensions.
Offline one-off. Packs the training-data CSV plus summaries/risks/retrospective-forecast JSONL into data/webapp.db (tables activity_info and mock_forecasts).
Offline one-off. Adds an embeddings table to data/webapp.db from activity_text_embeddings_gemini.jsonl, stored as float32 BLOBs so the DB is small enough to commit.
SQLite corpus database: activity_info, mock_forecasts, and embeddings tables. Built offline by the build_* scripts; read at runtime by sqlite_loaders.
Runtime reader of webapp.db. Supplies the BM25 retrieval corpus, activity-info lookups, and lazy mock forecasts that ground the narrative forecast.
Proxy for train_features.csv (see the ✳ node); loaded here by model_loader at startup.
Proxy for train_features.csv (see the ✳ node); the historical-activity rows that build_webapp_db packs into webapp.db to become the RAG retrieval corpus.
Proxy for train_features.csv (see the ✳ node); source of the validation metrics shown on the Model Performance page.
process_uploaded_pdf(): runs the 5-phase extraction on one PDF. Caches every output under projects/{activity_id}/ and reuses it on re-upload (activity_id = PDF content hash).
Phase 1/5: LLM extracts title, reporting/participating orgs, country locations, and dates.

Phase 2/5: LLM labels each PDF page as table_of_contents, blank_page, glossary, references, core_activities, theory_of_change, targets, broader_context, preliminary_results, other, condensed_summary, sub_activities_outlined, detailed_implementation_plans, broad_objectives, possible_outcomes, quantitative_targets, qualitative_targets, risks_as_word_or_numeric, risks_or_dangers_generally, plans_to_address_key_risks, positive_indicators, progress_reports, similar_cases_outcomes, implementation_context_country, contextual_challenges, financing_details, budget_and_legal, who_implements, whether_part_of_larger_program, partner_identity_or_skill, whether_skin_in_the_game, other_stakeholder_engagement, activity_monitoring_details
Phase 3/5: LLM writes an activity-description summary from the categorized pages.
Phase 4/5: LLM extracts a finance breakdown (allocations, total amount); a companion sector_extractor.py allocates expenditure across the model's sector clusters in the same phase.
Phase 5/5: LLM extracts baseline features: implementer performance, targets, risks, context, finance-qualitative, and complexity/integratedness.

Per-PDF extraction artifacts: uploaded.pdf plus metadata.json and implementer_performance.jsonl / targets.jsonl / risks.jsonl / context.jsonl / finance_qualitative.jsonl / misc.jsonl / page_categories.jsonl / summary.jsonl / finance_breakdown.jsonl. Written by the extraction phases; the PREDICT band later writes feature_grades.jsonl and targets_embedding_features.jsonl back here. Reused on re-upload of the same PDF.

Saved webapp session for this activity: all widget values, user edits, and the last prediction. Written to app_state_temp.json then app_state.json. Separate lifecycle from extraction: it persists and restores the user's working session, not PDF-derived data.
Saves and reloads a working session. Serialises st.session_state to app_state.json (autosaved via save_project_state_temp) and restores it into the page widgets on load.
LLM grades the extracted free-text features into the numeric scores the RF model expects.
Embeds targets/summary text and projects it through the saved UMAP models into embedding features.
Derives GDP / CPIA / governance and region indicators plus reporting-org dummies from the activity's location and organisation.

impute_and_run_statistical_model(): imputes the assembled feature vector, then combines a per-org-mode baseline with the RF + ExtraTrees delta ensemble (clipped to 0–5) plus a start-year drift correction to produce the overall success rating.
Per-outcome-tag classifiers predict probabilities for individual success dimensions.
Decomposes the RF prediction into signed per-feature contributions via tree_contributions.

Overall success rating on a 0–5 scale: per-org baseline + RF/ExtraTrees ensemble delta + start-year correction (base, ens_delta, prediction). Written by the forecast handler in page_activity_forecasting.py; held in Streamlit session_state.
Per-feature SHAP contribution breakdown plus base_value, from shap_explainer.compute_shap_values. Held in session_state; rendered as the contribution waterfall.
Per-outcome-tag probabilities {tag: p}, from tag_predictor.predict_outcome_tags. Held in session_state; rendered as the per-dimension tag panel.
Retrieves the most similar historical activities (BM25 by default, or Gemini-embedding cosine) to ground the narrative forecast.
Synthesizes an LLM narrative forecast of likely outcomes, using the RF prediction as a reference point and the retrieved neighbours as evidence.
Free-text outcome forecast with commentary, accumulated as log lines by run_rag_forecast and shown on the Narrative Forecast page.

Main page, now a thin orchestrator over the pages/activity_forecasting/ subpackage (project_selector, upload, inputs, sectors, grading, embeddings, analysis, tags): drives extraction + prediction and shows the rating, editable feature sliders, and SHAP annotations.
Displays the raw cached extraction outputs for the uploaded PDF.
Runs and displays the RAG narrative forecast.
Shows model accuracy / explained-variance metrics against the validation set.
Static informational pages: glossary of terms, project background, and a feedback form.
